pricing: The rate is confirmed before any paid session is booked. Sessions are per session, with no upfront commitments.
archive_note: Some PDFs and notes are historic teaching materials written by Dr Nicky Grant for previous teaching and do not represent current courses or current course content.

study_notes.note: Free study notes (blog) written from Dr Grant's own university lecture material, grouped by route and theme.

a_level_exam_board_guides.note: Board-specific A-Level exam guides (paper structure, assessment objectives and weightings, common mark losses, original worked exam-style questions). Part of the A-Level & Admissions route.

teaching_resources.note: Free teaching materials, mostly in the University & Postgraduate route. Downloadable lecture notes, exercises, problem sets and worked solutions in PDF and TeX, plus worked video lectures.
teaching_resources.downloads_pdf: https://www.drnickygrant.com/teaching/ (time-series and financial-econometrics lecture slides, notes, exercises and solutions)
teaching_resources.source_tex: https://www.drnickygrant.com/teaching/tex/ (TeX source for lecture slides, notes and problem sets)

videos.note: Free worked video lectures by Dr Grant on the @economaths YouTube channel, embedded and indexed on teaching.html. Teaching resources, not research outputs. Most relevant to the University & Postgraduate route (econometrics, time series, financial econometrics).

time_series_playlist.topics: stationarity, white noise, unit roots, ARIMA, autocorrelation, the correlogram, Newey-West (HAC) standard errors, cointegration

videos.applied_econometrics_videos: 30+ short worked videos on regression theory, matrix methods, asymptotics, inference, instrumental variables, heteroskedasticity, autocorrelation, ARIMA and applied techniques
